name: Florence VR: Climb Cathedral Bell Tower

reviews.review_description: Very disappointing. It's a 360° video from a head mounted camera. A person recording his visit to the bell tower of the cathedra of the Saint Marry of the flower. Walking from window to window multiple times, recording from behind the protective steal net. Then walking up the stairs. Level by level. Spending a bit of time on each floor, waking from window to window. Eventually getting to the very top. Waking around the top twice. Then going down and making a round trip around the cathedral. Worst of all is that video is not edited, no montage! And you can't fast forward it 😡 which means you get the FULL experience, that includes queuing on stairs and sitting in place for couple minutes every time this guy takes a break 🤦‍♂️. Video is poor quality on top of it (my internet connection is not an issue add other videos from YouTube it meta TV look crisp). Utter waste of time not to mention money. You're much better just watching a 3D VR video on YouTube.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the detailed review – even though we’re of course sorry to hear the app didn’t meet your expectations.

But what you describe is actually intentional: This is not a cut, fast-paced highlights video. It’s a real-time VR experience, filmed from a visitor’s point of view – including breaks, pauses, queueing, the changing light, the ambient sounds, and the full climb step by step. That’s what makes it feel real.

We’ve also improved the streaming quality significantly since release – especially for Quest 3 users with a stable connection.

reviews.review_description: You will find way better for free on YouTube. And not even a fast forward offer something

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your comment.
We hear your feedback – just a heads-up: The streaming quality has been significantly improved since launch, especially for Quest 3 users or anyone with a stable connection. What may have felt blurry before is now much clearer and more immersive.

This isn’t just a 360° video – it’s a deliberate VR experience, focused on presence and calm. You climb Florence’s bell tower step by step, feeling the shift in light and sound as if you’re truly there. It’s not about speed or effects – it’s about being in the moment.

reviews.review_description: The  majestic bell tower has ginormous proportions, viewed from outside and from inside. In this VR app, climbing the tower from the ground level to the summit, you have magnificent panoramic views from each level, especially from the summit. Views of city of Florence, laced with typical Tuscan roofs, under the Tuscan blue sky makes the tour really enjoyable.

developer_response.body:
Thank you, DartArt – we really appreciate your review!

You’ve captured exactly what this app is meant to deliver: the sense of real presence, without artificial speed or gamified elements. Just you, the architecture, and the quiet progression up Giotto’s majestic tower. From floor to floor, window to window, until Florence opens up beneath you.

We love how you highlighted the views and atmosphere under the Tuscan sky. That’s the emotional core of this VR experience: no filters, no distractions – just a moment of stillness at the top.

Thanks again for recognizing the quiet power of this kind of experience.
